↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇸🇦🇮🇷 Saudi air defences reported intercepting and destroying four ballistic missiles in the Eastern Region. @wfwitness" ⚡️ 🇸🇦 🇮🇷 Footage from the Missile impact in the industrial city of al-Jubail located within the eastern province of Saudi Arabia. @wfwitness

After a U.S. F-15E was shot down over Iran, both crew members were eventually rescued, but the operation became controversial due to a leak during the mission. Trump said the U.S. had only publicly confirmed rescuing one crew member, while keeping quiet about the second, who was wounded and hiding in mountainous terrain. According to Trump, a media report revealed that “there’s somebody missing,” which he claims alerted Iran that another American airman was still on the ground. Trump argued this compromised operational security, saying it made the rescue “much more difficult” and put both the missing airman and rescue forces at risk. He emphasized that once the information became public, Iranian forces—and even civilians—were aware and searching, especially after Iran reportedly offered a reward for capturing the pilot. The first public hint came from Israeli journalist Amit Segal, who bragged & posted: “Western source: One of the American crew members was successfully rescued.” This implied another crew member was still missing. Segal later said he was not sure he was first, but made clear he would not reveal his source. Trump responded by launching an investigation into the leak, calling the leaker a “sick person” and warning the media outlet involved to disclose the source or face consequences: “give it up or go to jail.”
Note : Saudi Arabia’s SABIC is the largest petrochemical company in the Middle East (West Asia) and the world’s fourth largest.

The U.S. carried out a B-2 bomber strike on an underground IRGC HQ in Tehran, dropping bunker-buster bombs during the rescue window. Ordered by CENTCOM commander Adm. Brad Cooper, multiple B-2s flew a 36-hour round trip from Whiteman Air Force Base, dropping massive bunker-buster bombs (the same type used against Iranian nuclear sites). The strike targeted a location where senior IRGC commanders were gathered, with U.S. officials saying the facility was destroyed and many commanders are presumed killed.

Iran submitted a 10-point proposal to end the war via Pakistan, demanding: 1- End of all attacks. 2- Full sanctions relief. 3- Halt to Israeli strikes (incl. Hezbollah). 4- Reconstruction support. 5- Secure Hormuz shipping. 6- Guarantees against future attacks. In return, Iran would reopen Hormuz (with a ~$2 million per ship fee). Source: NYT

Open Source Intel SecWar Hegseth says the U.S. kept a live coordination call running for nearly 48 hours for the American airman stranded in Iran. “The top of it read, 45 hours and 56 minutes. For 45 hours and 56 minutes, we held that call open for coordination!” “From the moment our pilots went down, our mission was unblinking. The call never dropped, the meeting never stopped, the planning never ceased.” “The President demanded rapid, decisive action.” “He backed our military with the full weight of his resolve, refusing to let enemy propaganda or tactical setbacks deter us.” tweet

WarTranslated The "Minudobreniya" chemical plant in Rossosh, Voronezh region, has been attacked. The OSINT community CyberBoroshno confirms that the main fertilizer storage facility is on fire, posing a risk of a massive explosion. This plant is a major producer of ammonium nitrate, which is used in the manufacture of both explosives and fertilizers. tweet
Iranian missiles reportedly hit Al Jubail in Saudi Arabia, causing a large fire. Al Jubail is the Middle East’s largest industrial hub, home to major petrochemical and energy facilities.

Iranian Light crude became more expensive than Brent (~+$1/bbl), a rare shift from its usual discounted price. This happened because war-driven supply shortages and limited sanctions relief made Iranian oil unusually valuable and in high demand.

The kill vehicle from a U.S. THAAD missile interceptor was recovered in southern Syria (Suwayda) after a recent interception. This component is the non-explosive part that destroys targets by direct high-speed impact (“hit-to-kill”). It likely fell back to Syria intact after completing or failing its mission, which is possible since THAAD carries no warhead.

Clear footage shows April 2 U.S. strikes on a bridge under construction in Karaj, Iran. Two groups of GBU-31 bombs hit the structure, causing part of the bridge to collapse.
Hezbollah drone operators are improving targeting, striking vulnerable points. One FPV drone targeted a weak spot on Israeli Merkava tank, while another hit the side of a parked Israeli Eitan APC.
🇮🇷 ⚡️ 🇮🇱 Iranian missile hit rates have risen from 3% in the first two weeks of the war to 27% now, according to the Institute for the Study of War and JPMAM.

Three U.S. personnel were injured during the Iran rescue mission: the downed F-15E weapons officer and two helicopter crew members hit by ground fire. Rescue helicopters came under intense small-arms fire, with one aircraft taking multiple hits, while the injured officer managed to move and signal his location despite severe wounds. Source: AP

Israeli National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir on new law mandating death by hanging for Palestinians: I’ve already taken everything from them. I also want to take the lives of the terrorists. This is right, just, and it will happen.

Israel has approved a new list of Iranian energy and infrastructure targets in case diplomacy fails, but is waiting for U.S. approval before acting. Israeli officials are skeptical a deal will work and are pushing for any ceasefire to require Iran to fully stop uranium enrichment and give up its enriched stockpile. Source: CNN

An Iranian drone strike on a U.S. air base in Kuwait injured 15 American personnel, most of whom have already returned to duty. Overall, 373 U.S. service members have been injured in the operation so far, with the majority back in service and five listed as seriously wounded. Source: CBS
